extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon won the stage 5 individual time trial, with BERNAL Egan climbing to second overall, 19 seconds behind yellow jersey KWIATKOWSKI Michał.
tactical_patterns: KWIATKOWSKI collecting 14 bonus seconds before the ITT reveals proactive Yellow Jersey defense against superior time trialists.
BERNAL finishing 15 seconds off ITT winner YATES shows Grand Tour TT competence earlier than typical Colombian climber development.
JUNGELS Bob 11th at intermediate check clawing back to 8th finish suggests pacing errors under pressure as pre-race favorite.
Education First placing four riders in top 10 ITT indicates team-wide aerodynamic optimization exceeding equipment-only explanations.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: first professional ITT victory on flat course contradicts pure-climber stereotype, peaks well pre-Giro
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: 15 seconds off ITT win plus crosswind strength stages 1-2 reveals complete stage racer toolkit at 22
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: third on stage extends lead but BERNAL at 19 seconds pressures remaining mountain stages
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: 17th on stage, over minute down overall confirms continued ITT weakness prevents stage race contention
rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: negative: slipped from second overall, losing 1:15 exposes time trial vulnerability despite strong road racing
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: negative: 8th finish as pre-stage favorite and Luxembourg TT champion shows form below spring expectations
rider_signals.POLITT Nils: positive: second place finish marks breakout ITT performance suggesting emergence as classics/TT specialist
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: positive: first of four Education First in top 10 demonstrates maintained TT ability despite declining GC trajectory
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: working hard on TT improvement but stage result still confirms discipline remains limitation for GC ambitions
